title: 33rd Maintenance Squadron Refurbishes Khobar Towers Memorial Sword
description: Airmen assigned to the 33rd Maintenance Squadron revitalized the flaming sword centered on the 33rd Fighter Wing's Khobar Towers Memorial by modifying it to keep the flame burning without environmental disruption.

body.text: Airmen assigned to the 33rd Maintenance Squadron have worked to revitalize the flaming sword centered on the 33rd Fighter Wing's Khobar Towers Memorial by modifying it to keep the flame burning without environmental disruption.

Air Force Senior Airman Adam Williams, 33rd Maintenance Squadron metals technology journeyman, poses in front of the newly refurbished sword on the Khobar Towers Memorial at Eglin Air Force Base, Fla., Feb. 20, 2026. Williams, who grew up in Saudi Arabia near the site of the Khobar Towers bombing, was selected to refurbish the sword.
